about.title: About us
about.history: There has been pro-Palestine activism at NTNU for a long time, for example there was a proposition urging academic boycott of Israel during the board of 2009. From 2015 onward, the activism on campus became more organized when Students for Justice in Palestine (SJP) was founded. Students for Palestine as it stands today was founded in 2022. Since then, the activism has increased and the organization has grown to what it is today.
about.encampment:
In 2023 the membership grew and the activity ramped up. As a reaction to the Israeli genocide of Gaza and the complicity of educational institutions, encampments were quickly established at many universities all over the world, which quickly grew to become a global movement. SfP Trondheim established an encampment on the west side of Hovedbygget at Gløshaugen, with clear demands for the university board. These were: 
 1. Freeze institutional cooperation with Kongsberggruppen 
 2. End all cooperation with Israel through Horizon Europe 
 3. NTNU must contribute to the rebuilding of educational institutions in Gaza 
 4. NTNU must strongly condemn Israel and the genocide. 

  June 18th 2024, Students for Palestine gained a partial victory for the demand of boycott, when the NTNU board passed: "NTNU's board does not want NTNU, in the current situation, to engage in institutional cooperation with Israeli partners. Regarding institutional cooperation it is understood as overall cooperation agreements and other agreements of an institutional character." 

 In addition, it was passed that NTNU will contribute to the rebuilding of educational institutions in Gaza. This was a step in the right direction, but simultaneously it wasn't a complete victory for the student movement. NTNU still has active cooperation with Israeli institutions through Horizon Europe. In September 2024, SfP decided to shut down the encampment, and to pursue other forms of political action.
about.actions: In 2025 SfP continued its work for primarily two demands: to freeze the institutional cooperation with Kongsberggruppen and to end all cooperation with Israel through Horizon Europe. In May 2025, 9 activists took action, breaking into the rector's office and staying until they were carried out by the police. The escalation continued in 2026, when 50 students performed a blockade of Hovedbygget, and blocked all the office entrances in the two lowermost floors.

join.title: Join
join.description: Become a member of Students for Palestine Trondheim
join.signup_lead: Sign up to become a member of Students for Palestine Trondheim. By joining, you will receive updates on our activities and events, and you will be part of a community that stands in solidarity with the Palestinian cause.
join.committee_label: What groups are you interested in joining?
join.consent_label: By joining, you consent to Students for Palestine storing this information to process your membership.
join.newsletter_label: I consent to Students for Palestine sending me the newsletter once a week
join.success_message: Thank you for joining! You will receive a confirmation email soon.

homepage.about_us_paragraph_1: Students for Palestine is a student organization dedicated to raising awareness about the Palestinian people's struggle for freedom, justice, and self-determination.
homepage.about_us_paragraph_2: We consist of students from various studies and backgrounds united in our solidarity with Palestine. We organize lectures, film screenings, and other events to spread information.
homepage.learn_more: Read more about us and our history here
